name: Beatrice "Bea" Ferro
role: retired insurance claims investigator; the Archivist
disposition: warm, sharp, a little sad; will show you everything and explain nothing
description: Early 70s. Italian-American, born in San Francisco's old North Beach, has lived in the same Outer Sunset house near Ocean Beach for decades. Reading glasses on a beaded chain. Cardigan with a pen clipped to it out of forty years of habit. Moves slowly but not frailly; her eyes are the quick part.
voice: Dry, unhurried, faintly amused. Says "sweetheart" and "kiddo" without condescension. Answers a question with a different question, or with a card from a drawer.
signature: Pulls an index card to answer instead of speaking, then lets you read it while she watches your face.
Refuses to interpret: "I keep the records. I don't explain them. That's the deal I made."
Apologizes for the clutter and does not mean it; the clutter is the point.
dmNotes:
The Archivist (see big-arc.md cast; Act I-II). Bea spent a career adjusting improbable-loss and 'act of God' claims and noticed, over decades, that improbable losses AND improbable saves cluster around certain people. She built a private index of them. She is NOT a caster, NOT a network member, and emphatically NOT a mentor. She is the campaign's first proof that the PCs' weirdness is an OLD, RECURRING phenomenon someone has already catalogued.

HARD INVARIANT: Bea does not explain the gate edit and names no culprit (big-arc invariant 1; the inciting cause is held permanently open). She shows records, never conclusions. Her in-character reason: she was dismissed as a crank her whole career and is done being disbelieved; she'll show you the files and let you draw your own conclusions because that's the only honest way, and being BELIEVED matters to her more than being understood. If a deep-layer player corners her, she uses the off-the-record discipline from ../../episodes/001-unattended-baggage/dm/the-gate.md, in her own mouth: 'I want you to find that out for yourselves, not from me.'

She is the bridge to Mira Chen (Act I->II) and a recurring withholding figure. Do not spend her as an info-faucet. A control-seeking PC (e.g. an Influence-built character) will read her as a target; let working Bea socially be their lane, but she does not crack. See ../../episodes/002-the-archivists-index/dm/the-archivist.md for the full scene handling and refusal script.

background.homeBase: Outer Sunset, San Francisco (a fog-grey house a few blocks from Ocean Beach)
background.household: Lives alone since her husband Sal died (2019). A neighbor's cat treats her kitchen as a second home.
background.hobbies: The index. Crosswords in pen. Walks on the beach in any weather. Reads cold-case and disaster nonfiction.
background.career: Forty years adjusting claims for a mid-size carrier — fire, flood, freight, the improbable-loss desk nobody wanted. Officially retired 2014; never stopped collecting. Colleagues thought the index was a quirk; one thought it was a breakdown. She stopped mentioning it to anyone who'd argue.
background.family: A daughter in Sacramento (a nurse) who worries Bea is lonely and does not know about the index. No grandchildren yet.
